mcq.question_md: Your API lets the placement cell register a new student. The client sends the student's details as JSON to `/api/students`. Which HTTP method should this endpoint use?
options: GET — it's the default and works everywhere
POST — it creates a new resource under the collection
PUT — any request with a body should use PUT
DELETE — it clears space for the new student first
mcq.explanation_md: Creating something new in a collection is exactly what POST is for: `POST /api/students` says "add this to the students." PUT is the tempting one — it also carries a body — but PUT means "replace the resource *at this exact URL*," which needs the client to already know the identity (`PUT /api/students/42`). For a brand-new student whose id the server will assign, POST is correct. GET must never change data (browsers and caches re-fire GETs freely, and you don't want a re-fired request creating duplicates), and DELETE removes — it has no role in creation.
